title: VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER - 211info
text: VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 808-1256 Main phone (877) 424-3838 Hotline Main phone (503) 808-1256 Hotline (877) 424-3838 Homeless Veterans INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for appointment. Call for outreach if unable to appear in person. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Benton, Clackamas, Clatsop, Columbia, Deschutes, Lincoln, Linn, Marion, Multnomah, Polk, Tillamook, Washington, and Yamhill Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: 1st and Oak Street MAX stop Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Homeless Veterans Benefits and Services Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Case/Care Management * Homeless Veterans Case/Care Management * Veterans General Clothing Provision * Homeless Veterans General Clothing Provision * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Veterans Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans Housing Search Assistance * Homeless Veterans Housing Search Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Veterans Personal/Grooming Supplies * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers * Homeless Veterans Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers * Veterans Specialized Information and Referral * Homeless Veterans Specialized Information and Referral * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Substance Use Disorder Referrals * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans Veteran Employment Programs Veteran Outpatient Clinics Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - VA SUICIDE PREVENTION TEAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
